title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. cognatum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. cognatum is a subspecies of the umbrella sage plant, a type of flowering shrub native to western North America, characterized by its clusters of small, white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. cognatum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. cognatum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
